items.title: RT Eknath Shinde - एकनाथ शिंदे: 📍 खालापूर | मुंबई-पुणे द्रुतगती मार्गाला पूर्णतः ‘ऍक्सेस क...
items.description_html:
RT Eknath Shinde - एकनाथ शिंदे
📍 खालापूर |

मुंबई-पुणे द्रुतगती मार्गाला पूर्णतः ‘ऍक्सेस कंट्रोल’ बनवणाऱ्या महत्त्वाकांक्षी प्रकल्प असलेल्या मिसिंग लिंक या प्रकल्पाची आज पाहणी केली. येत्या १ मे रोजी मुख्यमंत्री देवेंद्र फडणवीस यांच्याहस्ते या प्रकल्पाचे उद्घाटन होणार आहे.

मुंबई-पुणे ‘मिसिंग लिंक’ प्रकल्पाचे ९९ टक्के काम आता पूर्ण झाले असून, या प्रकल्पामुळे घाटातील वाहतूक कोंडी मोठ्या प्रमाणावर कमी होऊन प्रवास अधिक वेगवान आणि सुरक्षित होणार आहे. त्यामुळे नागरिकांची घाटातील वाहतूक कोंडीतून मुक्तता होऊन जलद दळणवळणामुळे अर्थव्यवस्थेलाही चालना मिळणार आहे.

१०.५ किलोमीटर लांबीच्या या प्रकल्पात दोन भव्य बोगदे, उंच व्हायाडक्ट आणि टायगर व्हॅलीवरील सुमारे १८२ मीटर उंच केबल-स्टेड पूल उभारण्यात आला आहे. सुमारे २३.७५ मीटर रुंद बोगदा हा जगातील सर्वात रुंद बोगद्यांपैकी एक मानला जातो. ७० किमी प्रतितास वेगाने वाहणारे वारे, मुसळधार पाऊस आणि कठीण भौगोलिक परिस्थितीत हा प्रकल्प यशस्वीरीत्या पूर्ण करण्यात आला आहे. लोणावळा तलावाच्या १८२ मीटर खालून हा रस्ता जाणार आहे, हा प्रकल्प पूर्ण करणे म्हणजे एक आव्हान होते, मात्र महाराष्ट्र राज्य रस्ते विकास महामंडळाने (MSRDC) हे मोठे आव्हान स्वीकारून प्रकल्प पूर्णत्वास नेल्याचे सांगत त्यांचे विशेष कौतुक केले.

मिसिंग लिंक प्रकल्प सुरू झाल्यानंतर टोलमध्ये कोणतीही वाढ होणार नाही. चारचाकी वाहने आणि बसेस यांना प्राधान्य देण्यात येणार असल्याचे यावेळी बोलताना स्पष्ट केले.

याप्रसंगी खासदार श्रीरंग बारणे, माजी नगरसेवक आबासाहेब बागुल, एमएसआरडीसीचे व्यवस्थापकीय संचालक अनिलकुमार गायकवाड तसेच एमएसआरडीसी, एफ्कॉन आणि नवयुग कंपनीचे सर्व प्रमुख अभियंते आणि कर्मचारी उपस्थित होते.

#Mumbai #Pune #MissingLink #MSRDC #EknathShinde #Maharashtra
@MPShrirangBarne

items.title: RT Parimal: In 1971, amidst the mud & blood of refugee camps, 1 man defied the entire medical world. While doctors begged for IV drips that did not ex...
items.description_html:
RT Parimal
In 1971, amidst the mud & blood of refugee camps, 1 man defied the entire medical world. While doctors begged for IV drips that did not exist, Dilip Mahalanabis mixed salt & sugar in plastic drums & told the dying to drink. He was a ghost who used the physics of the human gut to defeat Cholera. He never patented his formula, choosing to save millions instead of making millions. He died in 2022, an unrecognized giant who gave the world its most successful medicine for the price of a pinch of salt.

Born in 1934 in West Bengal, Dilip Mahalanabis was a pediatrician by training. He was a researcher at the Johns Hopkins University International Centre for Medical Research & Training in Calcutta. He spent his early career in the Silo of elite academic research, studying how fluids move through the human body. Despite his elite training, he was a man of the people. He did not want to stay in a comfortable air-conditioned hospital while a crisis was brewing.

During the 1971 Bangladesh Liberation War, millions of refugees poured into West Bengal. A cholera epidemic broke out in the camps. People were dying in heaps. There were not enough IV fluids/needles/trained doctors. The conventional medical wisdom said: "Only IV drips can save a cholera patient."

Dr. Mahalanabis knew the physics of S.N. De’s discovery. He knew that if he could get the concentration of salt & sugar exactly right, the gut would pump the water back into the body. He prepared the Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS) in massive drums, using untrained volunteers to distribute it to dying refugees.

The mortality rate dropped from 30% to 3%. He proved that a 5 paise solution was more effective than a 50 rupees IV bag. He never patented ORS. He believed that something so fundamental to human survival should belong to the world. Because there was no money/corporate patent attached to his name, the world used his work but forgot his face.

For decades, he lived quietly in Calcutta. He was a global consultant for the WHO, helping eliminate cholera in dozens of countries, yet in his own neighborhood, he was just the retired doctor from down the street.

He died in 2022. It was only after his death that many of his relatives & neighbors realized he was the man The Lancet described as the creator of the most important medical advance in the 20th century.

We finally awarded him our 2nd highest civilian honor, Padma Vibhushan, but it came after he passed away. He lived & died a Ghost.

items.title: RT Jakub Wiech: Mili Państwo, mogło Wam to umknąć, ale: w 2025 r. źródła odnawialne przebiły węgiel w światowej produkcji energii elektryczn...
items.description_html:
RT Jakub Wiech
Mili Państwo, mogło Wam to umknąć, ale: w 2025 r. źródła odnawialne przebiły węgiel w światowej produkcji energii elektrycznej.

🌍Jak podaje Ember, stało się tak po raz pierwszy od 100 lat, ale w zupełnie innej skali niż wiek temu. W zeszłym roku OZE wygenerowały łącznie 10 730 TWh (33,8% światowej produkcji prądu), a węgiel 10 476 TWh (33%).

Za 1/3 globalnego miksu generacyjnego odpowiadają już zatem źródła odnawialne.

Ale to nie wszystko. Spójrzcie na resztę najnowszych danych:

🔥Udział węgla w globalnej produkcji prądu spadł do najniższego poziomu w historii;

☀️W 2025 roku aż 75% światowego wzrostu zapotrzebowania na energię elektryczną pokryło jedno źródło - fotowoltaika. Pozostałą część pokryły inne źródła bezemisyjne;

📈Ubiegłoroczny wzrost produkcji energii elektryczności z samych tylko paneli słonecznych wyniósł 636 TWh - to mniej więcej 4 razy tyle, ile rocznie zużywa Polska. Z kolei całość globalnej produkcji z PV sięgnęła 2778 TWh, co oznacza wzrost o 100% od 2022 roku i o 900% w porównaniu do roku 2015;

📉Gwałtowny rozwój OZE sprawił, że w ubiegłym roku produkcja prądu z paliw kopalnych spadła rokrocznie o 0,2%. To pierwszy taki spadek od pandemii.

Jak się zsumuje OZE i atom to wychodzi, że wkrótce połowa globalnej produkcji prądu będzie już bezemisyjna. To jest nasz wielki sukces.

items.title: .@_HannahRitchie, our Deputy Editor and Science Outreach Lead, has won the 2026 Unwin Award! The award recognizes “non-fiction writers in the earlier...
items.description_html:
.@_HannahRitchie, our Deputy Editor and Science Outreach Lead, has won the 2026 Unwin Award!

The award recognizes “non-fiction writers in the earlier stages of their careers as authors, whose work is considered to have made a significant contribution to the world.”

It’s awarded for an author’s overall body of work. Hannah has written two books:

– Clearing the Air: A Hopeful Guide to Solving Climate Change in 50 Questions and Answers
– Not the End of the World: Surprising facts, dangerous myths and hopeful solutions for our future on planet Earth

The award’s judging panel praised "Not the End of the World" as “a well-written and revealing book and for its optimistic and data-grounded approach which gives readers hope for the future of the planet.”

The award comes with a £10,000 prize, which Hannah decided to donate to the Against Malaria Foundation.

Congratulations, Hannah!

Read more in the award announcement: https://www.publishers.org.uk/hannah-ritchie-wins-unwin-award-2026/

items.title: RT www.sidin.co: Carefully wading into the abroad versus India debate for one second. IMO it all depends on two factors: 1. What do you want to take f...
items.description_html:
RT www.sidin.co
Carefully wading into the abroad versus India debate for one second.

IMO it all depends on two factors:
1. What do you want to take for granted in life? (Work, house, labour, schools, parks, food, govt., whatever) Different places offer different things you can take for granted. And this dramatically changes with income.
2. And how do you see those assumptions changing through the life of your children to adulthood. Great now can be bad in ten years. Bad now can be great in 15.

It is ok to have your own solution to this equation.

items.title: RT Sumita Kale: And for all those who are truly interested in the impact of heat stress, beyond X wars of development/environment arrows, please read ...
items.description_html:
RT Sumita Kale
And for all those who are truly interested in the impact of heat stress, beyond X wars of development/environment arrows, please read this excellent report from @Artha__Global and Centre for Rapid Insights that flags the impact of rising heat in neighourhoods of Delhi, a very granular study
Kudos to the team @pritika13 Dr Neelanjan Sircar @priya_vedavalli @CafeEconomics

"The findings show that even small differences in built-up form and vegetation correspond to meaningful differences in experienced heat. Increasing built-up area from roughly 25% to 55% raises experienced temperature by about 0.6°C. In contrast, increasing tree cover from around 3% to 11% lowers experienced heat by approximately 1°C. This asymmetry suggests that even modest improvements in green cover have a stronger cooling effect than the warming effect associated with similar increases in built-up area. The result reinforces the role of neighbourhood-scale greening as a practical lever for reducing heat exposure in dense urban settings."

https://artha.global/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Artha-Global_Climate-Study-Report_Final-A4_Single-Page-View-1.pdf

items.title: RT Sridhar Vembu: Open letter to Indians in America. -- Dear brothers and sisters from Bharat: Like I did 37 years ago, you arrived in America with no...
items.description_html:
RT Sridhar Vembu
Open letter to Indians in America.
--
Dear brothers and sisters from Bharat:

Like I did 37 years ago, you arrived in America with no money but with a good education and cultural heritage from Bharat. You achieved outstanding success. America was good to us. For that we must remain grateful - gratitude is our Bharatiya way.

Yet today, a significant number of Americans, may be not the majority but not too far from it either, believe that Indians "take away" American jobs and our success in America was unfairly earned.

You may think the next election will fix this, but your choice would be between people who hate our Bharatiya civilisation and people who hate civilisation itself. That is the "hard right" vs "woke left" battle. You are mere bystanders to that conflict.

Meanwhile there is one thing that is true now and will be true in the future: the respect Indians command world-wide will substantially depend on the fortunes of India herself. If India remains poor, the woke left will give us moral lectures with pity and the hard right, different moral lectures with scorn ("hellhole") and we must not confuse either with respect.

Respect in today's world, along with prosperity and security, comes from one source: a nation's technological prowess. India produces sufficient brain power to achieve that prowess but alas we exported so much of that talent, particularly to America. As we develop that prowess in India, our civilisational strength will assert itself.

As difficult as it is for many of you to contemplate this, please come back home. Bharat Mata needs your talent. Our vast youthful population needs the technology leadership you gained over the years to guide them towards prosperity. Let's do it with a missionary zeal.

Respectfully
Sridhar Vembu

items.title: RT Lukasz Olejnik: Research shows that Chinese AI can reliably detect software vulnerabilities - and it is cost efficient. Kimi K2.5, an open-weight m...
items.description_html:
RT Lukasz Olejnik
Research shows that Chinese AI can reliably detect software vulnerabilities - and it is cost efficient. Kimi K2.5, an open-weight model was deployed in an agentic framework against Chrome and produced 10 previously unknown zero-days, including two critical sandbox-escape CVEs. The researchers noted it was cheaper to run than Claude Opus 4.6 at that scale. The most important word in the paper isn't "zero-day" -- it's "cheaper"? https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.20801

items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
Happy to see my work in @thewire_in

GST’s key feature: it’s self-enforcing. Firms need invoices to claim credits → they demand compliance from suppliers → formalisation cascades through supply chains.

https://thewire.in/economy/how-the-gst-created-a-formalisation-cascade

from @Ideas4India

Paper
https://meghapatnaik.github.io/research/gst_prowess.pdf

Thrilled to share our paper on Sustainable Remote Workplaces (with @BeataJechorek , @asdurso & @ChloeThurstonDC ) is forthcoming in JSIS.

#FutureOfWork

items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: new blog post at @Ideas4India 45% of India’s manufacturing is informal. My work shows GST is slowly changing this: an input credit ...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
new blog post at @Ideas4India 45% of India’s manufacturing is informal. My work shows GST is slowly changing this: an input credit system creates a formalisation cascade where firms demand invoices from suppliers & this pulls the entire supply chain slowly into the formal economy


Ideas for India: Value-added tax has a self-enforcing design that generates formalisation cascades through supply chains. Using @_CMIE Prowess data on 12,024 firms for the period 2013-2022, Megha Patnaik @megonomista examines impact of India’s introduction of GST in 2017
https://www.ideasforindia.in/topics/macroeconomics/how-the-gst-created-a-formalisation-cascade

items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: Global Capability Centres are projected to contribute to 3.5% of India's GDP. My new paper argues that GCCs are firm-level reorganis...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
Global Capability Centres are projected to contribute to 3.5% of India's GDP. My new paper argues that GCCs are firm-level reorganisation at the global scale: only the most productive firms set up GCCs, trading off lower wages against higher coordination costs of remote work.


CEPR: New CEPR Discussion Paper - DP21294
Services Across Borders: How Firms Organize Remote Work at Scale
Megha Patnaik @megonomista @UniLUISS
https://ow.ly/2gIf50YuL4m
#CEPR_ITRE #EconTwitter

items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: 45% of India's GDP is informal. My new paper shows GST's credit mechanism is pulling firms into the formal economy, not through enfo...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
45% of India's GDP is informal. My new paper shows GST's credit mechanism is pulling firms into the formal economy, not through enforcement but because every buyer demands an invoice from the supplier behind it.
Formalisation cascading begins with large firms and grows upstream


CEPR: New CEPR Discussion Paper - DP21163
Self-Enforcing Tax Design and Supply Chain Formalization: Evidence from India’s GST Reform
Megha Patnaik @megonomista @UniLUISS
https://ow.ly/5QK550YfcL1
#CEPR_PE #EconTwitter

items.title: RT Pramod P: Re Finally, there is some truth to the issue that usage of organized sector as proxy for unorganised is not fully valid. E.g. Agriculture...
items.description_html:
RT Pramod P
Re Finally, there is some truth to the issue that usage of organized sector as proxy for unorganised is not fully valid. E.g. Agriculture is independently measured without reference to corporates. Similarly sectors like Construction and Transport while it uses organized sector indicators (Vehicles, Cement, Steel etc) for measurement, these inputs are mostly produced in the organized sector and would be a valid way of measurement.

items.title: RT Pramod P: Re The author's point that usage of CPI instead of WPI for some of the services sectors would cause around 0.3% average less growth would...
items.description_html:
RT Pramod P
Re The author's point that usage of CPI instead of WPI for some of the services sectors would cause around 0.3% average less growth would be right and there is some compensatory offset effects due to higher deflators for Communications equipment manufacturing (electronics prices reduce usually even with an increase in quality) and communication services may lead to some underestimaton.

items.title: RT Pramod P: Re 2. Sectors like Trade and Other services have also significantly reduced in size in the new series proving the point that the old seri...
items.description_html:
RT Pramod P
Re 2. Sectors like Trade and Other services have also significantly reduced in size in the new series proving the point that the old series overstated growth.

items.title: RT Pramod P: Re 1. There is an increase in size in agriculture and Ownership of dwelling (notional entry mostly). This masks the reduction in size of ...
items.description_html:
RT Pramod P
Re 1. There is an increase in size in agriculture and Ownership of dwelling (notional entry mostly). This masks the reduction in size of other industries in household sector. However, the Private Corporate sector includes something called Quasi corporates which are effectively informal sector as well. Private corporate sector has reduced in size as per the new series.

items.title: RT Pramod P: Prof. Goldars one primary argument that the old methodology of using organized sector growth as proxy for informal sector didn't cause mu...
items.description_html:
RT Pramod P
Prof. Goldars one primary argument that the old methodology of using organized sector growth as proxy for informal sector didn't cause much overestimation rests on the case that Household sector size didn't change much even in the new 2022-23 series. However, few caveats:


Ideas for India: Some scholars have put forth that India’s GDP growth rate was overestimated in the old national accounts series with base year 2011-12, due to methodological issues. Goldar refutes these arguments; concludes there was likely no significant overestimation

https://www.ideasforindia.in/topics/macroeconomics/was-indias-growth-rate-overestimated-in-the-old-national-accounts-series

items.title: Jefferies on steel 🔍 1. China’s output cuts (-8% YoY) are shrinking exports (-13% YoY in Mar), tightening supply and lifting Asian spreads 2. Conv...
items.description_html:
Jefferies on steel 🔍

1. China’s output cuts (-8% YoY) are shrinking exports (-13% YoY in Mar), tightening supply and lifting Asian spreads

2. Conversion spreads are rebounding from ~15-year lows, with more upside as China balance improves

3. India steel prices +20% CYTD (helped by 12% safeguard duty) and could rise further to ~₹65.8K vs ₹58K spot if global spreads normalize.

4. Earnings upgrades likely for JSW Steel & Tata Steel with strong EPS sensitivity to price gains.

JSW Steel remains the top Buy!

@CNBCTV18News @CNBCTV18Live

items.title: The correct answer here is to not vote at all. Most people in the world won't vote because this is a silly assumption that they will. Three choices: n...
items.description_html:
The correct answer here is to not vote at all. Most people in the world won't vote because this is a silly assumption that they will.

Three choices:
no vote assumed to be red: most ppl are red, survive.
No vote=blue, survive
No vote= unaffected, so survive


Tim Urban: Everyone in the world has to take a private vote by pressing a red or blue button. If more than 50% of people press the blue button, everyone survives. If less than 50% of people press the blue button, only people who pressed the red button survive. Which button would you press?

items.title: India doesn't need a swap line. It has 600bn$ nearly, to sell, if it wants and sadly it doesn't want to. Cad is 50bn to 75bn a year. There is no exit ...
items.description_html:
India doesn't need a swap line. It has 600bn$ nearly, to sell, if it wants and sadly it doesn't want to. Cad is 50bn to 75bn a year.

There is no exit tax. 20% above 10L a year is only for lrs and is advance tax that can be adjusted even against salary tds.

And no mf inv was limited in 2009, not now. We didn't increase limits

Lrs for investment is tiny tiny tiny
Compared to travel. And even education. People are not lining up to take out money other than a few want to believe on twitter

All figments of imagination. We should sell a lot more dollars. And at least now try to build trade in inr.


Somnath Mukherjee: Idiots + imagined facts = Influencers. Ver 101👇

items.title: India’s 60+ age population is projected to rise from ~142 million in 2021 to 231 million by 2036, taking its share in the total population from ~10% ...
items.description_html:
India’s 60+ age population is projected to rise from ~142 million in 2021 to 231 million by 2036, taking its share in the total population from ~10% to ~15%. By 2036, the specialised senior care market could be ~USD 35 billion per annum.

items.title: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Hormuz: “If what they mean by opening the straits is, ‘yes, the straits are opened, as long as you coordinate w...
items.description_html:
US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Hormuz:

“If what they mean by opening the straits is, ‘yes, the straits are opened, as long as you coordinate with Iran, get our permission, or we will blow you up and you pay us,’ — that’s not opening the straits,” Rubio told Fox News.

“They cannot normalize — nor can we tolerate them trying to normalize — a system in which the Iranians decide who gets to use an international waterway and how much you have to pay them to use it.”

items.title: RT وزارة الخارجية: حضرة صاحبِ الجلالة السلطان هيثم بن طارق المُعظم - حفظه الله ورعاه...
items.description_html:
RT وزارة الخارجية
حضرة صاحبِ الجلالة السلطان هيثم بن طارق المُعظم - حفظه الله ورعاه - يستقبل بقصر البركة العامر، معالي الدكتور سيد عباس عراقجي وزير خارجية الجمهورية الإسلامية الإيرانية ، جرى خلال المقابلة التشاور حول مستجدات الأوضاع في المنطقة، وجهود الوساطة والمساعي الرامية إلى إنهاء النزاعات، حيث اطّلع جلالةُ السلطان على وجهات نظر الجانب الإيراني حيال تلك التّطورات.

فيما استمع معالي الوزير إلى مرئيات جلالته بشأن سُبل الدّفع بهذه الجهود بما يعزز فرص التوصل إلى حلول سياسيّة مُستدامة ويحدّ من تداعيات الأزمات على شعوب المنطقة، مؤكدًا جلالةُ السلطان على أهمية تغليب لغة الحوار والدّبلوماسية في معالجة القضايا، بما يُسهم في ترسيخ دعائم السّلام.

من جانبه أعرب معالي الدكتور وزير الخارجية الإيراني عن تقدير بلاده لمواقف سلطنة عُمان بقيادة جلالتِه في دعم جهود الحوار وتعزيز مساعي الأمن والاستقرار في المنطقة، خاصة في ظل التحدّيات الإقليميّة الرّاهنة.

items.title: President Trump has canceled the trip by his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Islamabad due to the Iranian position in peace negotiations, he...
items.description_html:
President Trump has canceled the trip by his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Islamabad due to the Iranian position in peace negotiations, he told @Axios.

"I see no point of sending them on an 18-hour flight in the current situation [of the negotiations]. It's too long. We can do it just as well by telephone. The Iranians can call us if they want. We are not gonna travel just to sit there," the president said.

items.title: The market is bracing for a volatile midterm election year: Since 1896, the Dow Jones Industrial Average has posted an average return of just +1.2% fr...
items.description_html:
The market is bracing for a volatile midterm election year:

Since 1896, the Dow Jones Industrial Average has posted an average return of just +1.2% from May 1st through Halloween of a midterm election year.

In the following winter months from Halloween to May 1st, the index has seen an average return of +10.5%, or +9.3 percentage points higher.

By comparison, excluding midterm election years, the DJIA has returned an average of +2.2% during summer months and +3.5% during winter months.

In other words, this narrows the gap to just +1.3 percentage points outside of midterm election years.

History suggests a volatile summer is ahead.

items.title: Nvidia is making history again: Nvidia, $NVDA, now represents 4.96% of the MSCI All Country World Index (ACWI), near the highest level on record. The ...
items.description_html:
Nvidia is making history again:

Nvidia, $NVDA, now represents 4.96% of the MSCI All Country World Index (ACWI), near the highest level on record.

The ACWI Index covers ~85% of global equity markets, including large and mid-cap stocks.

Nvidia’s weight is now larger than Japan’s proportion of 4.94%, the world’s 3rd-largest stock market.

Its contribution to the index is also greater than that of France and Germany combined.

By comparison, the UK, Canada, and China make up 3.30%, 3.09%, and 2.83%, respectively.

There has never been a company like Nvidia.

items.title: China has maintained massive crude inventories despite Middle East supply disruptions: Chinese crude inventories have fallen by less than 1 million ba...
items.description_html:
China has maintained massive crude inventories despite Middle East supply disruptions:

Chinese crude inventories have fallen by less than 1 million barrels since the Iran War began on February 28th.

As a result, total Chinese crude oil inventories still stand at ~1.8 billion barrels, including strategic reserves.

Since March 2025, inventories have risen +400 million barrels, or +29%.

This comes as cheap Iranian and Russian crude continues to flow into China, while the country also introduced measures to suspend fuel exports to keep domestic supplies stable.

Chinese purchases of Iranian crude are set to rise to a record ~1.9 million barrels per day this month.

China has the world’s largest oil buffer and is using it carefully.

items.title: American economic confidence is now below April 2020 levels: The Gallup Economic Confidence Index declined -11 points in April, to -38, the lowest rea...
items.description_html:
American economic confidence is now below April 2020 levels:

The Gallup Economic Confidence Index declined -11 points in April, to -38, the lowest reading since November 2023.

This is now below the pandemic low of -33 points seen in April 2020.

To put this into perspective, the all-time high was +56 points in January 2000, while the all-time low was -72 points in October 2008.

Currently, 73% of Americans believe the economy is getting worse, the highest since May 2023, while only 23% believe it is getting better.

Furthermore, only 33% of Americans say it is a good time to find a quality job, the lowest reading since January 2021, down from a peak of 74% in October 2021.

Americans are very concerned about the economy.

items.title: BREAKING: Over 560 Google, $GOOGL, employees have signed an open letter to the CEO urging him refuse to let the US government use its AI technology fo...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Over 560 Google, $GOOGL, employees have signed an open letter to the CEO urging him refuse to let the US government use its AI technology for classified military operations.

"We want to see AI benefit humanity, not being used in inhumane or extremely harmful ways," read the letter, which was sent today.

This comes just weeks after Anthropic was designated a "supply-chain risk" after the company refused to give the government unrestricted access to its AI models.

Google could become the next AI giant to face US government scrutiny.

BREAKING: Canada is launching an $18.4 billion sovereign wealth fund to "bankroll major projects of national interest," per FT.

Details include:

1. This marks the first ever sovereign wealth fund for Canada and is is part of Carney’s drive to turn Canada into the “strongest economy of the G7”

2. The “Canada Strong Fund” will work alongside the private sector to finance 15 infrastructure proposals

3. This comes as Canada’s deficit jumped to C$78 billion in 2025, up from $42 billion the previous year

4. Carney looks to catalyze $1 trillion in total investment in Canada over the next 5 years

The move comes Canada has dealt with US tariffs for over one year.

items.title: The semiconductor sector is taking over the market: Semiconductor stocks now represent a record 16.1% of the S&P 500's market cap. The percentage has ...
items.description_html:
The semiconductor sector is taking over the market:

Semiconductor stocks now represent a record 16.1% of the S&P 500's market cap.

The percentage has TRIPLED since the 2022 bear market.

This is also now 11 percentage points above the 2000 Dot-Com Bubble peak.

By comparison, defensive sectors, including energy, healthcare, staples, and utilities, make up 19.2% of the S&P 500 index.

This is 4 percentage points below the previous low of 23.0%, set in 1999.

At this pace, the weight of the semiconductor sector will surpass that of defensive sectors by as early as next year.

Semiconductor stocks are reshaping the investing landscape.

4. President Trump said the Iranian position led him to cancel a trip by his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan

Indirect US-Iran talks appear to still be ongoing.

items.title: Tech dominance is now a global trend: The information technology sector now reflects 85% of Taiwan's stock market cap, the most among major world mark...
items.description_html:
Tech dominance is now a global trend:

The information technology sector now reflects 85% of Taiwan's stock market cap, the most among major world markets.

This is followed by South Korea and the Netherlands, at 57.2% and 54.6%, respectively.

The US is ranked 4th, with IT making up 32.7% of the S&P 500's total market value.

Including communication services, technology stocks reflect a near-record 45% of the US stock market, still below the top 3 countries.

Meanwhile, the weight of global tech stocks as a % of the total global market ex-US stands at ~11.0%, the highest since 2021 but below the 14% peak during the 2000 Dot-Com Bubble.

Global equity markets have rarely been this concentrated in a single sector.

items.title: White collar employment is sharply declining: The number of the S&P 500 employees fell -400,000 in 2025, to 28.1 million, posting its first annual dec...
items.description_html:
White collar employment is sharply declining:

The number of the S&P 500 employees fell -400,000 in 2025, to 28.1 million, posting its first annual decline since 2016.

This follows 8 consecutive years of uninterrupted employment growth, adding over +3.0 million jobs in total.

The decline was driven by UPS, $UPS, Oracle, $ORCL, Amazon, $AMZN, Meta, $META, Intel, $INTC, and Microsoft, $MSFT, as corporations raced to cut costs and redirect spending toward AI.

In 2026, layoffs are set to continue with Amazon cutting ~16,000 corporate jobs, Meta slashing ~8,000 positions, and Microsoft offering voluntary buyouts to ~8,750 employees.

Corporate America is cutting jobs at an accelerating pace.

items.title: Food inflation is set to accelerate in the US: Average inflation for food and beverage companies surged +7.9% YoY in March, the biggest jump in at lea...
items.description_html:
Food inflation is set to accelerate in the US:

Average inflation for food and beverage companies surged +7.9% YoY in March, the biggest jump in at least 12 months.

This is up +373 basis points from +4.2% in February.

The largest jump was recorded in tomatoes at +102% YoY, vegetables at +90%, and diesel at +88%.

This was largely driven by higher fuel costs, meaning the full impact of rising fertilizer and plastics prices has not yet been reflected.

Meanwhile, urea prices, the world's most widely used nitrogen fertilizer, have doubled since February, to ~$900 per metric ton, the highest since the 2022.

Fertilizer costs are rapidly rising for farmers and will eventually translate into higher wholesale food prices before landing on grocery store shelves.

Food inflation is set to accelerate.

items.title: RT Jim Bianco: This story produced a red screaming headline on Bloomberg. *IRAN OFFERS US NEW PROPOSAL TO REOPEN STRAIT OF HORMUZ: AXIOS The markets e...
items.description_html:
RT Jim Bianco
This story produced a red screaming headline on Bloomberg.

*IRAN OFFERS US NEW PROPOSAL TO REOPEN STRAIT OF HORMUZ: AXIOS

The markets excitedly pumped on the headline.

The problem is that this story was public 11 hours earlier.
https://x.com/BabakVahdad/status/2048411197930709497?s=20

Effectively, this is a totally unrealistic proposal. Still, the administration waited 11 hours until markets were open to hype so-called "good news" by calling an American (or Israeli) reporter to say "A DEAL IS COMING!" and breathlessly include "We'll meet in the situation room to discuss."

What is amazing is how desperate the market wants this to be the case, and how it bounces every time it happens.


Barak Ravid: NEW: Iran gave the U.S. a new proposal for reaching a deal on the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and lifting the naval blockade first, and postponing nuclear negotiations for a later stage. My story on @axios
https://www.axios.com/2026/04/27/iran-us-hormuz-strait-nuclear-talks-proposal-pakistan

items.title: IEX Concall Updates Coal Exchange opportunities: Large demand on buy side and sell side with multiple sellers and merchant mines. Govt pushing for E-a...
items.description_html:
IEX Concall Updates
Coal Exchange opportunities:
Large demand on buy side and sell side with multiple sellers and merchant mines.
Govt pushing for E-auction coal to trade through exchanges.
In the Beginning stages, buyers will have to make arrangements to lift the coal themself. Price discovery, coal quality to be ensured.
MCX also filed for Coal exchange but only derivatives, IEX to be spot market.
TAM can be told only after final regulations.

Operations side:
More than 70% trading is through API. Price discovery is through algorithm and using AI for only creating new products and operational improvement.

Gas exchange:
Volumes not as good as expected, due to middle east crisis. Q1 we may not grow, but definitely in Q2 we will see growth. Domestic + other suppliers too trading here.
Deadline to reduce stake in IGX extended to Dec 31st 2026.

Market Coupling:

When asked for a timeline, management says “can't say when final notification will be issued”.
On the new draft about Grid India deciding price, management says “situation is fluid”.
“They might review this decision or may not go ahead at all.”
“All exchanges have their own software. Grid India has to create infrastructure, software and connect all these exchanges. Bids will be collected and submitted to Grid India. I don't think so any value creation is happening.
No benefit of coupling”
As for trading participant’s response to coupling, no change in their working. Customer loyalty is very important. We interact with them, teach them how to use software, data analytics, faster bidding, settlement support, so a lot of value addition from IEX. I am sure we will retain our customers. Even if coupling happens, we will retain our market share. Liquidity will continue. We don't expect any significant margin drop.
RTM no case of market coupling anywhere. Tight timeline 15 minute and 5 minutes time block. Not possible to implement.
Renewable suppliers and battery suppliers number increasing everyday.

FY27 volume growth will be maintained at 15-20% growth. This year will have high demand.

CFD contracts pilot launch for 500MW fixed price battery capacity.

items.title: Finally some good progress on Market Coupling - Round Robin system is no more - Grid India takes Operator status to match bids as a neutral party - Gr...
items.description_html:
Finally some good progress on Market Coupling

- Round Robin system is no more
- Grid India takes Operator status to match bids as a neutral party
- Grid India has 6 months to come up with the Market coupling algorithm similar to Europe's Day Ahead Market
- Post 6 months, then after everyone's approval they have to build the systems, the infrastructure, cybersecurity protection etc etc

Looks like this should take 12 to 18months to see some value

This is assuming IEX does not put new cases on the order to stall

Now contrary to popular belief - Market Coupling like the EU is actually good

- EU trading Market boomed post market Coupling
- The industry leader EPEX Spot who was a market leader before coupling still has most of the volume
- EU has only 2 players taking all the market share with Nord Pool being 2nd

IEX
- Yes market coupling without round Robin is good, but market coupling itself will take some market share
- At best market share should dip from 99% to 75%.
- At best post market coupling they would stall revenues for 1 year
- Post market coupling the regulator will boost Power trading a lot
- Volume will rise
- News instruments like CfD coming to power exchanges

Verdict
Short term IEX will be volatile
For long term investors the game will start post Market Coupling

Today Indias largest Power exchange is at close to a billion dollars.

Disc - Invested in IEX at Lower Levels. Not a buy or sell reco

4QFY26: All moving parts align in a seasonally strong quarter

Strong growth momentum; profitability to improve gradually
Asset quality shapes up well across segments in a seasonally strong quarter

UBS on AU Small Fin BK
Neutral, TP Rs 1150
Strong quarter; Asset Quality/NIMs improve sharply
4Q: PAT beat driven by higher NII and lower provisions
Business growth healthy, margins expanded 26bp QoQ
Management reiterates RoA guidance of 1.8%; credit cost to trend lower

Jefferies on AU Small Fin BK
Buy, TP Rs 1250
AU Bank's Mar-Qtr profit of Rs8.3bn, up 65% YoY, was ahead of est., aided by significantly lower credit costs.
AUM grew 21% YoY, aided by strong momentum in wheels & commercial bk.
NIMs rose 24bps QoQ but may fall in coming quarters as banks raised peak deposit rates by 15-25bps.
Transition to Universal Bank is a key opportunity to aid growth and lift ROA.
See ROA rising to 1.7% in FY27E

CITI on AU Small Fin BK
Buy, TP Rs 1225
4Q PAT surging 65% YoY/25% QoQ to register RoA of 1.8% — decisively ahead of expectations.
NIMs expanded a robust 24bps QoQ to 5.96%, decomposed as: 12bps CoF tailwind, 7bps day-count benefit, and 6bps from lower slippage drag.
Notwithstanding a Rs210mn contingency provision, credit cost sustained its normalization trajectory, compressing sharply to 0.84% (vs 1.11% in 3Q), underpinned by unsecured as well as secured segments.
Mgmt reiterated confidence in sustaining 1.8% RoA on a full-year basis in FY27 (vs 1.6% in FY26), anchored by resilient growth momentum and an intensified efficiency drive.

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: CLSA on RBL BL Hold, TP Rs 320 A tough quarter with 4QFY26 PBT c.20% below estimate Miss was driven by both PPOP and credit costs. On ...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
CLSA on RBL BL
Hold, TP Rs 320
A tough quarter with 4QFY26 PBT c.20% below estimate
Miss was driven by both PPOP and credit costs.
On PPOP front, while loan growth was good, NII growth was tepid.
NII grew just 1% QoQ, despite double-digit loan growth, driven by a 50bp decline in loan yields.
Credit costs were sequentially unchanged at 2.5%, driven by continued pressure on asset quality in credit cards
Only good thing in quarter was growth – both on loans and deposits.
Deposit growth of 16% QoQ had some transient, quarter-end deposit flows – adjusted for that, it would have been 12% QoQ
Loan book also grew 11% QoQ.

CITI on RBL BK
Buy TP Rs 390
RoA held flat QoQ at 0.55%, missing estimates due to a 22bps NIM contraction to 4.41% (vs CitiE of 4.64%) & elevated credit costs of 2.5%.
Yield compression of 49bps was driven by repo repricing, liquidity drag, asset mix shifts, and interest reversals.
Mgmt. guides NIMs to remain flattish in 1QFY27 before gradually recovering, supported by capital infusion.
Credit card stress persists — elevated slippages/write-offs are expected for two more quarters before normalizing in 2H — though JLG stress has meaningfully eased.
Advances grew a strong 11% QoQ, led by wholesale and secured retail, while unsecured retail remained subdued.
While growth trajectory was encouraging with contained cost, NIM and credit cost pressures weighed on RoA.
FY27E earnings revised down 3% on lower NIM and fee assumptions.
Key catalysts: ENBD equity infusion and credit cost normalization in 2H.

@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh @latha_venkatesh

items.title: AI capex remains one of the most critical variables to track over the next few quarters Next big test for the AI investment cycle will be upcoming ear...
items.description_html:
AI capex remains one of the most critical variables to track over the next few quarters

Next big test for the AI investment cycle will be upcoming earnings guidance from the hyperscalers starting 29 April

Capex as a percentage of Operating Cashflow for the four major US hyperscalers is expected to rise sharply from ~41% in 2023 to ~84% by 2026E

Their capex directly drives demand for semiconductors, servers, networking, power equipment & data center infrastructure

items.title: RT Parimal: He worked in a lab so small & hot that his sweat would often ruin his notes. He did not build a bomb/a satellite, but he solved a puzzle t...
items.description_html:
RT Parimal
He worked in a lab so small & hot that his sweat would often ruin his notes. He did not build a bomb/a satellite, but he solved a puzzle that was killing millions. In 1959, from a Silo in Calcutta, Sambhunath De discovered the secret pump that drains human life. He is the father of ORS: the ghost behind the most successful medical intervention in history. He was nominated for the Nobel by the world’s greatest giants, yet he died in 1985 traveling by local bus, unrecognized by the very people whose children he had saved.

Born in 1915 in a small village in West Bengal, S.N. De did not come from a family of elite scientists. He worked at the Nil Ratan Sircar (NRS) Medical College, Calcutta. While elite scientists were building rockets, De was working in a tiny, cramped lab with barely any ventilation. He did not have high-end sensors. He used his own intuition & rudimentary tools to study how certain invisible forces acted on human cells.

S.N. De solved the mystery of Cholera, but in a way that was pure Fluid Physics & Biophysics. For a century, the world thought Cholera was a blood infection. In 1959, in his tiny lab, De proved it was a toxin that attacked the fluid-transport mechanisms of the gut. He discovered the Cholera Toxin (CT). He demonstrated how the toxin created a pump that sucked water out of cells, a masterclass in osmotic pressure & molecular transport.

This discovery is the direct reason why ORS (Oral Rehydration Salt) exists. If we/anyone we know has ever been saved by a packet of ORS, we owe our life to S.N. De.

In 1954, Nobel Laureate Joshua Lederberg wrote to the Nobel Committee saying that S.N. De’s work was worthy of the prize. He was nominated multiple times, but like many Indian scientists, he was a Ghost in a colony. The prize never came.

In 1978, the Nobel Foundation organized a symposium on Cholera. They realized the man who started it all, S.N. De, was still alive in Calcutta. They invited him, & he arrived at the high-end gala in a simple suit, looking like a retired clerk.

He was a man of aggressive humility. He lived in a small house, traveled by local buses, & never sought patents for his discovery. He wanted the Signal (the cure) to be free for the world. His own family knew him as a dedicated doc who went to the lab every day. They had no idea that Nobel Prize winners in Europe & America were referencing his 1959 paper as 1 of the most important scientific documents of the 20th century.

items.title: RT Nithin Kamath: I recently had dinner with Dr Devi Shetty, the founder of Narayana Hospitals. For those who don't know him, he's the guy who figured...
items.description_html:
RT Nithin Kamath
I recently had dinner with Dr Devi Shetty, the founder of Narayana Hospitals. For those who don't know him, he's the guy who figured out how to do open heart surgery for a few hundred dollars when the same procedure costs a bomb in the US. Narayana has 18,000 beds across India, and if you ask most middle-class people in Bangalore about it, they'll speak highly of it.

There was one thing I kept thinking about over and over again after meeting him.

Narayana's market cap is around ₹38,000 crore. Now compare that to pretty much any half-decent financial services business in India, and it'll be valued more than that, including Zerodha. A brokerage, worth more than a hospital chain, that has probably saved hundreds of thousands of lives.

I get the arguments. If you're a fund manager/analyst, you can immediately explain it away using margins, capex, asset-light vs asset-heavy, and all that, and I'm not saying the market is wrong.

But it's still a strange world we've built, where the businesses closest to money get valued the highest, and the ones doing the hard and essential things get priced like boring utilities. A hospital carries physical infrastructure, enormous liability, thin margins and the actual weight of keeping people alive. And somehow that's worth less than a platform for buying and selling stocks.

I don't have a clean take on this. All of this just felt odd.

Ps: Nothing here is investment advice. For that, go to @zerodhavarsity

items.title: RT Riaz Hamidullah: My dear RAGHU bhai (@RaghuRai1), I won't ever hear your heartfelt voice again over phone, greeting me in immaculate Urdu and filli...
items.description_html:
RT Riaz Hamidullah
My dear RAGHU bhai (@RaghuRai1), I won't ever hear your heartfelt voice again over phone, greeting me in immaculate Urdu and filling void with your signature warmth!

Last (2025) April, within two weeks of joining in Delhi, a call comes to me from him, "Reyaz bhai, I dare address you as 'bhai', hope you would grant me that allowance...". He sends his book, BIRTH OF A NATION, thru his daughter (based on all rare negatives of 1971 War that he misplaced in the pile of his archive).

He was one of my childhood heroes. Many #youth who may not know: in August 1971, a 29-year-old photo-journalist in @TheStatesmanLtd, Raghu Rai, entered #Bangladesh to cover 🇧🇩Liberation War. He shared with me accounts of intense suffering, agony and humiliation of #people that he witnessed in battlefield - villages - Refugee Camps. Raghu bhai narrated to me a chilling account of an ambush when he kept clicking even braving splinters!  

What he saw on the ground reminded him of his childhood when his family had to cross over to India (1947) braving so much of uncertainties. He was barely 5, then.
In 1972, 🇮🇳 Govt. awarded a young Raghu Rai #PadmaShri for his epochal photography on 🇧🇩War.

Since April '25, Raghu bhai embraced me just as a younger brother. A giant of man in the world of #photography #art #aesthetics, each time I would call him, he would greet me on phone almost like hugging me. Words just won't suffice.

Raghu bhai, in eight decades, you touched so many souls, across lands. You didn't belong just to #India but to the world.
Adieu, Raghu bhai. RIP.

@IndiaToday

items.title: RT Jyoti Yadav: On the morning after her wedding in 2008, Asha Devi was told she had to leave Gowardaha village for Mainpuri district in Uttar Pradesh...
items.description_html:
RT Jyoti Yadav
On the morning after her wedding in 2008, Asha Devi was told she had to leave Gowardaha village for Mainpuri district in Uttar Pradesh, nearly 750 kilometres away. She discovered then that her husband Lita Manjhi and father-in-law, Lalo, were bonded labourers — and that she too had been “supplied” as one.     

“Pata chala ki sasur aur pati karja mein khat raha hai (I learnt that they were trapped in debt),” recalled Asha, now 40. “Aise karke humko bhi pakad liya(That’s how I got dragged into it),” she added, sitting on a cot outside her thatched mud hut. 

Forty years ago, her father-in-law had taken a 5,000-rupee loan from Ramashish Prasad Yadav, which he was still paying back in 2008.

My ground report for @TMigrationStory

https://themigrationstory.com/post/the-inheritance-of-debt-bihars-musahars-still-marry-give-birth-in-bondage/

items.title: RT Gaurab Chakrabarti: Stella Chemifa started making fluoride compounds for toothpaste. Today they produce hydrofluoric acid at 12N purity: 99.9999999...
items.description_html:
RT Gaurab Chakrabarti
Stella Chemifa started making fluoride compounds for toothpaste. Today they produce hydrofluoric acid at 12N purity: 99.9999999999%. TSMC, Samsung, Intel, and SK Hynix depend on it to etch every advanced chip on earth. In July 2019, Japan restricted hydrogen fluoride exports to South Korea. Shipments dropped 87.9% in a month. Korea produces over half the world's memory chips, and had no alternative supplier at that purity. South Korea poured billions into domestic production. For the most advanced nodes, they still import from Japan. Getting from five nines to twelve nines took Stella Chemifa decades of process refinement. Every extra nine means ten times fewer contaminants. The global chip supply chain runs through 723 people in Osaka operating on toothpaste chemistry.

items.title: RT Aditya Kalra: ⚖️ 🍔 India's Zomato drops a price parity clause from its contracts after a revolt by restaurants who said it undermined their of...
items.description_html:
RT Aditya Kalra
⚖️ 🍔 India's Zomato drops a price parity clause from its contracts after a revolt by restaurants who said it undermined their offline pricing decisions.

⚖️Zomato was empowered earlier to use mystery shopping to detect any lower-than-app prices, according to Reuters review of these contracts

⚖️Five Lawyers and a former Indian antitrust official who reviewed the clause for @Reuters flagged it as anti-competitive.

⚖️What did the clause say? "Zomato may use various means including but not limited to customer complaints and mystery shopping to identify price disparity between a Restaurant's offline (dine-in) menu prices, and the prices on the Platform. The Restaurant Partner agrees that this amount will be deducted from its ongoing payouts." And it explained "Price Disparity": "The Restaurant Partner will at all times maintain equal or lower prices for all its products offered to the Customers via the Platform as against the prices through its direct channels"

READ. https://www.reuters.com/world/india/indian-food-delivery-giant-zomato-drops-pricing-clause-after-pushback-source-2026-04-23/

And for all those who are truly interested in the impact of heat stress, beyond X wars of development/environment arrows, please read this excellent report from @Artha__Global and Centre for Rapid Insights that flags the impact of rising heat in neighourhoods of Delhi, a very granular study
Kudos to the team @pritika13 Dr Neelanjan Sircar @priya_vedavalli @CafeEconomics

"The findings show that even small differences in built-up form and vegetation correspond to meaningful differences in experienced heat. Increasing built-up area from roughly 25% to 55% raises experienced temperature by about 0.6°C. In contrast, increasing tree cover from around 3% to 11% lowers experienced heat by approximately 1°C. This asymmetry suggests that even modest improvements in green cover have a stronger cooling effect than the warming effect associated with similar increases in built-up area. The result reinforces the role of neighbourhood-scale greening as a practical lever for reducing heat exposure in dense urban settings."

https://artha.global/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/Artha-Global_Climate-Study-Report_Final-A4_Single-Page-View-1.pdf

items.title: Mahindra Lifespaces delivered strong FY26 results with revenue growth of 216.5% YoY and PAT growth of 386.4% YoY, though the lumpy nature of project-b...
items.description_html:
Mahindra Lifespaces delivered strong FY26 results with revenue growth of 216.5% YoY and PAT growth of 386.4% YoY, though the lumpy nature of project-based revenue recognition under Ind AS 115 makes quarterly comparisons less meaningful.

The company executed a major balance sheet transformation through a ₹1,495 Cr rights issue, reducing debt-equity from 0.76x to 0.18x. However, cash conversion remains weak (CFO/PAT at -1.83x) and the business heavily relies on JV contributions, with standalone normalized PAT at only 2.6% of consolidated. FY27 pre-sales guidance of ₹4,500-5,000 Cr implies 26.7% CAGR target.

Positive Factors

FY26 revenue grew 216.5% YoY to ₹1,178.31 Cr

FY26 PAT grew 386.4% YoY to ₹298.13 Cr, normalized PAT grew 208.2% YoY to ₹188.89 Cr

Debt-equity ratio improved to 0.18x from 0.76x after ₹1,495 Cr rights issue

Non-current borrowings reduced to zero from ₹91,766 Lakhs

Net worth more than doubled to ₹3,626.87 Cr from ₹1,896.11 Cr YoY

Residential PAT turned positive for first time in 9M in Q3 FY26

FY26 finance costs decreased 43.7% YoY to ₹10.91 Cr

Risk Factors

Operating cash flow negative at ₹(544.93) Cr with CFO/PAT at -1.83x indicating poor cash conversion

Core standalone operations remain loss-making at normalized PAT of ₹4.89 Cr (2.6% of consolidated)

Working capital consumption high at ₹432.58 Cr (inventory build-up)

Operating margin (company-defined) remains negative at (9.6%) for FY26

Q4 FY26 PAT declined 17.2% QoQ despite revenue surge

Heavy reliance on JV contributions (₹348.91 Cr) for consolidated profitability

items.title: Maruti delivered strong top-line growth with revenue up 28.2% YoY in Q4 and beat export volume guidance by 12% (447,774 vs 400,000 units). However, pr...
items.description_html:
Maruti delivered strong top-line growth with revenue up 28.2% YoY in Q4 and beat export volume guidance by 12% (447,774 vs 400,000 units). However, profitability declined 6.4% YoY as margin compression of 148 bps from severe commodity cost pressure offset revenue gains.

Working capital deteriorated with inventories up 64% YoY, though free cash flow remained strong at ₹8,667 Cr. The company met all key operational guidance for FY26.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 28.2% YoY in Q4 to ₹52,463 Cr and 19.9% for FY26 to ₹1,83,316 Cr

Exports beat guidance: 447,774 units vs 400,000 target, growing 34.6% YoY

Company outperformed industry growth estimate: 8.43% vs ~7% estimated

Capex met guidance at ₹10,396 Cr vs ~₹10,000 Cr target

Free cash flow strong at ₹8,667 Cr for FY26

Operating cash flow grew 18.2% YoY to ₹19,063 Cr

Risk Factors

PAT declined 6.4% YoY in Q4 to ₹3,659 Cr despite strong revenue growth

EBITDA margin contracted 148 bps YoY to 11.9% in FY26

Cost of materials surged 50.7% YoY in Q4, as % of sales increased to 67% from 57%

Inventories surged 63.8% YoY to ₹11,315 Cr, indicating potential production-offtake mismatch

Other income fell 68% YoY in Q4 and 13.2% for full year

Effective tax rate rose to 25.6% in Q4 from 20.5% in Q4 FY25

items.title: Dhanlaxmi Bank delivered an exceptional Q4 with PPOP nearly tripling (194% YoY) and PAT growing 50% YoY. The turnaround story is confirmed with Corpor...
items.description_html:
Dhanlaxmi Bank delivered an exceptional Q4 with PPOP nearly tripling (194% YoY) and PAT growing 50% YoY. The turnaround story is confirmed with Corporate Banking swinging to profit, cost-to-income improving by 2,254 bps to 55.65%, and asset quality reaching healthy levels with NNPA at 0.51%.

No management guidance was provided in the source, so surprise magnitude is none, but the execution quality is superior with multiple operational beats.

Positive Factors

Total Income grew 30.13% YoY to ₹5,123 Cr in Q4 FY26
PPOP surged 193.87% YoY to ₹1,137 Cr in Q4, nearly tripling

Cost-to-income ratio improved to 55.65% from 78.19% (2,254 bps improvement)

GNPA improved 109 bps to 1.89%, NNPA improved 48 bps to 0.51%

CAR improved to 18.92% from 16.12% YoY

Advances grew 24.7% YoY to ₹14,918 Cr

Retail Banking revenue grew 66.86% YoY to ₹3,408 Cr

Corporate Banking swung to profit of ₹11 Cr from loss of ₹3 Cr

Risk Factors

Tax expense swung to ₹35 Cr expense from credit of ₹2 Cr in Q4 FY25, moderating PAT growth

Corporate Banking revenue declined 16.65% YoY

Borrowings increased 268.3% YoY to ₹737 Cr, indicating increased reliance on wholesale funding

Credit-Deposit ratio elevated at 80% vs 74.7% prior year

items.title: Dalmia Bharat delivered a strong Q4 with 21% sequential revenue recovery and margin inflection after three quarters of decline. Normalized PAT growth ...
items.description_html:
Dalmia Bharat delivered a strong Q4 with 21% sequential revenue recovery and margin inflection after three quarters of decline. Normalized PAT growth of 55% YoY demonstrates solid execution, though ROCE at 6.3% remains far below the 14-15% target.

The 88% reduction in PMLA case liability removes a major overhang, while capacity expansion to 75 MnT by FY28 provides long-term growth visibility. Cash conversion remains strong with FCF positive despite elevated capex.

Positive Factors

Q4 FY26 revenue grew 21.1% QoQ to ₹4,245 Crore with strong sequential recovery

Normalized PAT grew 55.4% YoY to ₹1,084.4 Crore in FY26

EBITDA margin expanded to 21.25% in Q4, up 408 bps sequentially from Q3

Operating cash flow at 1.97x PAT (₹2,278 Crore vs ₹1,157 Crore PAT)

Free cash flow positive at ₹222 Crore despite ₹2,056 Crore capex

PMLA case Proceeds of Crime reduced by 88% to ₹93 Crore, de-risking legal overhang

Net Debt/EBITDA at 0.46x, well below management target of <2.0x

Cost discipline: total expenses grew 1.9% vs revenue growth 5.9% in FY26

Risk Factors

ROCE at 6.27% is significantly below management target of 14-15%

Capex spending at ₹2,056 Crore was 24% below the revised guidance of ₹2,700 Crore

Revenue growth of 5.9% YoY slightly below industry volume guidance of 6-7%

Q4 PAT declined 11% YoY despite sequential recovery, muted YoY performance

One-time tax benefit of ₹75 Crore inflated reported PAT, normalized ETR around 25%

West Bengal incentive scheme of ₹250 Crore retrospectively revoked, new contingency

items.title: IFCI's Q4 FY26 showed strong top-line growth (19% YoY) and NII turnaround, but PAT collapsed 92% due to a massive provisioning swing. The company rema...
items.description_html:
IFCI's Q4 FY26 showed strong top-line growth (19% YoY) and NII turnaround, but PAT collapsed 92% due to a massive provisioning swing. The company remains in a critical financial health situation with CRAR at -18.78% and GNPA at 95.79%.

No management guidance exists for comparison. The consolidated entity outperforms standalone significantly, with subsidiaries driving group cash flows. The alpha opportunity is limited given severe capital adequacy breach and asset quality issues.

Positive Factors

Total income grew 19.22% YoY to ₹265.51 Cr in Q4 FY26

NII turned positive at ₹18.89 Cr in Q4 FY26 vs negative ₹48.06 Cr in Q4 FY25

PPOP grew 168.6% YoY to ₹109.83 Cr in Q4 FY26

Finance costs declined 24.43% YoY to ₹101.98 Cr

Consolidated PAT grew 5.75% YoY to ₹180.87 Cr

CRAR improved from -23.04% to -18.78%

Risk Factors

PAT declined 92.16% YoY to ₹21.36 Cr in Q4 due to provisioning swing of ₹440 Cr

CRAR remains deeply negative at -18.78%,
significantly below RBI minimum of 15%

GNPA ratio critically high at 95.79%, Net NPA at 78.44%

Standalone operating cash flow negative at -₹66.56 Cr for FY26

Normalized PBT declined 97.5% YoY after adjusting for one-time ARC gain

Loan book declined 12.5% YoY to ₹1,170.19 Cr

items.title: THELEELA delivered a strong FY26 with revenue growth of 17.4% and PAT surge of 743%, driven by significant deleveraging (debt down 60%) and cost disci...
items.description_html:
THELEELA delivered a strong FY26 with revenue growth of 17.4% and PAT surge of 743%, driven by significant deleveraging (debt down 60%) and cost discipline (operating leverage). The balance sheet transformation with Net Debt/Equity at 0.2x and strong cash conversion (1.93x PAT) provides significant financial flexibility.

Acquisitions in Coorg and Dubai signal expansion intent. Q4 was impacted by lower other income but full-year EBITDA met guidance. The company is well-positioned for sustained growth with a robust balance sheet.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 17.4% YoY to ₹15,273 Cr with strong operating leverage (expenses up only 11.1%)

PAT surged 743% YoY to ₹4,031 Cr driven by 55.6% reduction in finance costs and lower tax rate

Total debt reduced by 60% to ₹15,568 Cr; Net
Debt/Equity improved from 1.0x to 0.2x

Operating cash flow strong at ₹7,767 Cr (1.93x PAT); FCF positive at ₹3,827 Cr

Acquired Coorg resort (₹5,592 Cr) and 25% stake in Sofitel Dubai (JV profit ₹32 Cr)

EBITDA grew 16.1% YoY to ₹8,133 Cr, meeting mid-high teens guidance

Risk Factors

Q4 EBITDA growth muted at 3.3% YoY due to 79.7% decline in other income (from ₹381 mn to ₹77 mn)

Other income declined 33.5% YoY as cash deployed and debt repaid post-IPO

Subsidiaries less profitable (32% of PAT) due to higher debt and depreciation from expansion projects

Q4 EBITDA margin contracted 590 bps YoY to 56.4% due to lower other income base

items.title: Castrol India delivered revenue growth of 8.7% YoY beating its 6-7% guidance, but earnings growth lagged at 3.7% YoY missing the 6-7% EPS guidance. Ma...
items.description_html:
Castrol India delivered revenue growth of 8.7% YoY beating its 6-7% guidance, but earnings growth lagged at 3.7% YoY missing the 6-7% EPS guidance. Margin compression of 424 bps QoQ (from 25.55% to 21.31%) due to rising raw material costs and 33% YoY increase in employee costs offset the top-line beat.

The EV fluids contract win and sustained double-digit industrial growth provide future growth optionality, but near-term profitability remains under pressure from input cost headwinds.

Positive Factors

Revenue grew 8.7% YoY to ₹1,545.24 Cr, beating management guidance of 6-7%

Industrial business sustained double-digit growth

Won new contract with leading EV two-wheeler manufacturer

Finance costs declined 19.7% YoY indicating near debt-free status

Total comprehensive income grew 4.1% YoY to ₹242.96 Cr

Risk Factors

PAT grew only 3.7% YoY despite 8.7% revenue growth due to margin pressure

EBITDA margin contracted 424 bps QoQ to 21.3% from 25.55% in Q4 FY25

Employee costs rose 33.4% YoY, significantly faster than revenue

Raw material costs as % of revenue jumped to 49.6% from 42.8% in Q4 FY25

EPS growth of 3.8% missed management guidance of 6-7%

items.title: Trishakti Industries Limited || Q4'26 Investor Presentation INDIA'S INFRASTRUCTURE SUPERCYCLE Massive infrastructure push: Government spending on infr...
items.description_html:
Trishakti Industries Limited || Q4'26 Investor Presentation

INDIA'S INFRASTRUCTURE SUPERCYCLE

Massive infrastructure push: Government spending on infrastructure is rising fast (about 3x growth), with a strong focus on roads, railways, metros, and energy.

Roads lead the growth: Road construction is seeing the biggest boost (~12x multiplier), making it the largest driver of projects and demand.

Spillover to industries: This push supports sectors like steel, ports, and renewable energy, creating large opportunities for construction and equipment businesses.

THE CRANE TAM - X6.95L CR RE INVESTMENT =%11,300-17,500 CR OPPORTUNITY

Big growth ahead: India plans to grow renewable energy from ~147 GW today to 500 GW by 2030, mainly in solar and wind.

Huge investment needed: Around ₹23 lakh crore total investment, with solar and wind taking the largest share.

Large crane market opportunity: Building these projects will create a ₹11,300–17,500 crore demand for cranes and equipment.

items.title: Deepinder Goyal on the impact of AI chatbots on Zomato and Blinkit: “The people who are excited about experimenting with ordering food through AI ass...
items.description_html:
Deepinder Goyal on the impact of AI chatbots on Zomato and Blinkit:

“The people who are excited about experimenting with ordering food through AI assistants are a real but early segment, and currently nowhere close to disrupting our business.”

He adds that, just as Google was not able to disrupt consumer behaviour in travel booking (Expedia, Booking) and shopping (Amazon), general AI chat interfaces will not be able to disrupt “complex, high-frequency” transactions or the “brand associations” tied to specific apps.

“Our moat remains the physical world. AI makes that moat wider by allowing more of India to participate in it.”

More in the letter on how Eternal is using and thinking about AI.

items.title: Growth in large e-commerce platforms in India is increasingly being driven by catering to lower average order values (AOV). Zomato has seen net order ...
items.description_html:
Growth in large e-commerce platforms in India is increasingly being driven by catering to lower average order values (AOV).

Zomato has seen net order value (NOV) growth improve from 13.1% YoY in the June 2025 quarter to 18.8% in the March 2026 quarter.

The key drivers include lowering the free delivery threshold to Rs 99 from Rs 199 and pushing meals priced under Rs 250.

This has increased the number of monthly transacting customers (MTCs) by over 20%, from 20.9 million to 25.4 million over the last nine months.

“Order volume growth is being driven by MTC growth, both from new-to-platform customers and higher frequency among existing budget-conscious customers.”

Deepinder Goyal says that this has decreased AOV, but revenue per order continues to improve.

items.title: RT Aaron Levie: If you read this and don’t understand why it’s happening it’s an opportunity to reset your understanding of how the real world work...
items.description_html:
RT Aaron Levie
If you read this and don’t understand why it’s happening it’s an opportunity to reset your understanding of how the real world works.

The real world will need a ton of help actually getting agents going in the enterprise. Companies have legacy tech stacks they need to modernize, data in tons of fragmented tools, knowledge that isn’t captured or digitized, and change management needed to actually utilize agents effectively. And they have to do all this while still running their business day-to-day, unlike startups.

This is why there is so much opportunity for companies (software or services) to actually deploy agents in specific domains and workflows. This remains a big opportunity for both existing services providers but also tons of new startups as well. Every new technology wave produces a new era of consulting firms that can deliver on that technology.

It’s also why the FDE model is going to be alive and well for a long time because companies will want to have their vendor actually help drive the change management and implementation for their new workflows.

The people aren’t going away. Far from it.

It has been a common belief that somehow "offshore" dollars, including the dollar reserves held by non-US authorities, have to "leave" the US through US current account deficits. This belief is dead wrong.
Maurice Obstfeld explains: https://www.piie.com/blogs/realtime-economics/2026/dont-blame-americas-current-account-deficit-dollar
